cards.front:
Enable DHCP Snooping on the switch:
SW1(config)# [...]

Enable DHCP Snooping on a VLAN:
SW1(config)# [...]

Enable errdisable recovery for DHCP rate limiting violations:
SW1(config)# [...]

Configure the DHCP snooping-enabled switch to not add option 82 to DHCP messages:
SW1(config)# [...]

Configure a DHCP snooping trusted port:
SW1(config-if)# [...]

Limit the rate that DHCP messages are allowed on a DHCP snooping enabled interface:
SW1(config-if)# [...]

Show the DHCP snooping binding table:
SW1# [...]
When DHCP snooping is enabled, all interfaces are [trusted/untrusted] by default.

DHCP Server or DHCP Client message?
OFFER

DHCP Server or DHCP Client message?
ACK

DHCP Server or DHCP Client message?
NAK

DHCP Server or DHCP Client message?
DISCOVER

DHCP Server or DHCP Client message?
REQUEST

DHCP Server or DHCP Client message?
RELEASE

DHCP Server or DHCP Client message?
DECLINE

DHCP snooping untrusted port:
If a DHCP [...] message is received, discard it with no further checks.

DHCP snooping untrusted port:
If a DHCP [...] message is received, inspect the message to determine if it should be forwarded or discarded.
DHCP Snooping: When a client successfully leases an IP address from a server, create a new entry in the [...]
If DHCP snooping rate-limiting is configured and the rate is exceeded, what happens?
DHCP Option [...] is also known as the DHCP Relay Agent Information Option.
By default, Cisco switches will drop DHCP messages with Option 82 that are received on an [...] port.
